title: Agent Traces, GenAI Semantics, Token Usage, Cost, Privacy, and Diagnosis · Language Clinic: Trace Hierarchy
summary: Logs contain full prompts and secrets but omit tool arguments, approvals, retries, model version, token usage, cost, and causal links needed to diagnose a failure. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the trace hierarchy increment in privacy-safe reconstructable agent telemetry. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chan…
objectives: Correlate service request, session, invocation, interaction, model call, tool call, approval, retry, effect, and recovery spans and events.
Give deterministic safety and policy failures precedence, retain metric disagreement, report uncertainty, and escalate high-impact ambiguous cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-observability-token-cost-3
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-observability-token-cost-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def worked_agentpy_observability_token_cost_3(invocations: int = 40, traced_invocations: int = 40, usage_records: int = 40, redacted_records: int = 40, unbounded_content_logs: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorityworked3",
        "interaction5798",
        "tool3dekrow",
        "stateworked35798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if invocations < 1 or traced_invocations < 0:
        return False, traced_invocations, "trace-count"
    if traced_invocations != invocations or usage_records != invocations:
        return False, usage_records, "telemetry-gap"
    if redacted_records != invocations:
        return False, redacted_records, "telemetry-redaction"
    if unbounded_content_logs > 0:
        return False, unbounded_content_logs, "content-log-leak"
    assert traced_invocations == invocations and usage_records == invocations
    return True, traced_invocations, "observable-agent-operations"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Build Trace Hierarchy evidence · Theory Trace Hierarchy
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-trace-hierarchy-rilhwc" that uses case 7042 to emit reconstructable agent, model, tool, approval, retry, token, latency, cost, error, and privacy-safe trace evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-trace-hierarchy-rilhwc
# Competency: agentpy-trace-hierarchy.
# Case 7042: an accessible public-service intake assistant.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates.
def evidence_agentpy_trace_hierarchy_rilhwc(invocations: int = 40, traced_invocations: int = 40, usage_records: int = 40, redacted_records: int = 40, unbounded_content_logs: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_rilhwc = "rilhwc-cwhlir-7042"
    assert evidence_variant_rilhwc
    evidence_axes_rilhwc = (
        "authorityrilhwc",
        "interaction7042",
        "toolcwhlir",
        "staterilhwc7042",
        "evaluation7042rilhwc",
        "recoverycwhlir7042",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_rilhwc)) == 6
    if invocations < 1 or traced_invocations < 0:
        return False, traced_invocations, "trace-count"
    if traced_invocations != invocations or usage_records != invocations:
        return False, usage_records, "telemetry-gap"
    if redacted_records != invocations:
        return False, redacted_records, "telemetry-redaction"
    if unbounded_content_logs > 0:
        return False, unbounded_content_logs, "content-log-leak"
    assert traced_invocations == invocations and usage_records == invocations
    return True, traced_invocations, "observable-agent-operations"
